Telemarketing Script for User’s Choice
Where You Get Through to the Decision Maker
(When You’ve Already Been in the Area and
Got a Strong or Weak Referral)
Receptionist: Hello, ____________ Construction. Can I help you? (If the receptionist
says his or her name, then repeat it after you say, “Hi” in the following response.)
(Weak Referral)
BD Agent: Hi, my name is ________________, and I’m a state compliance advisor for
(your companies name). I was in your area the other day, meeting with _________, over
at ___________________, and he told me to give you guys a call. Who’s the right person
to speak here about employee training and development? Would that be the head of your
HR department or is it someone else?
(Strong Referral)
BD Agent: Yeah, is _____________ there?
Receptionist: Can I tell him who’s calling?
BD Agent: It’s (Your first name), from (your companies name)? I got his number from
__________, over at ____________. Can I speak to him, please?
Receptionist: Okay, I’ll put you through.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 4 --

HR Person: Hello, this is ________________.
BD Agent: Hi, _______________. This is _____________, and I’m a state compliance
advisor for a registered training organization called (your companies name). Mate, I was
in your area last week working with ________________ over at _________________,
and told me to give you a call. He said that you were the man to speak to over here about
training employees. Is that the case, mate?
HR Person: Yes, it is.
BD Agent: Okay, great! Well, __________, the reason for the call today is that the state
has just started to replace the old Workplace Health & Safety system with nationally
recognized trade qualifications … and my company, (your companies name), has a state
contract that provides you with 100% funding to get your people their new
qualifications. Now, ______________, how many employees do you currently have on
staff right now, either in the field or the warehouse, mate … just a ballpark?
HR Person: About ____ people.
BD Agent: Okay, great! And what sorts of jobs do they do, mate? You have anyone on
road construction … pipe laying … commercial driving … or anything else like that?
HR Person: (Gives you a breakdown.)
BD Agent: Okay, great. And how about their employment status? Can you give me a
rough breakdown as to how many of them are casual, permanent part time, and also
permanent full time … just a ballpark?
HR Person: (Gives you a breakdown.)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 4 --

BD Agent: Okay, great. Well, ________, let me say this: based on everything you’ve just
said to me, the program we have at (your companies name) is definitely a perfect fit for
you. Now, what I’d like to do now, with your permission, is send you out some basic
information on my company, via email, and also brief description of how the program
works, and then get back to you in the next few days or so to discuss things in more
detail. Sound fair enough?
HR-person: Yeah, sure.
BD Agent: Okay, great! Now, let me just make sure I have all your correct info. Now,
first:
1) 	What’s your best email address? Do you have one for the company or would you
prefer I use your personal one?
2) 	And what’s your mobile number, mate?
3) 	And what’s the best way to usually reach you, on your mobile or your office
phone?
4) 	And the time of day, mate? Are you usually free in the mornings or afternoons?
5) 	Okay, great! And, lastly, the address I have on file for your company is
____________. Is that correct, mate? Is that address you work at?
BD Agent: Okay great! You know, I’m actually going to be up in your area on
_________ and __________ of next week, and I’d be more than happy to stop by in
person and discuss things in more detail. Which day is better for you, mate, ________ or
_________?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 4 --

HR Person: __________ works.
BD Agent: Okay, perfect. And what time would you like, morning or afternoon? I’m
flexible either way.
HR Person: _____________ is better.
BD Agent: Okay, great! How does _______ o’clock sound? Does that work?
HR Person: Yes.
BD Agent: Okay, great. I’ll put that in my calendar right now. And I’ll also put my
mobile number in the email as well, so if you have any questions at all before that, feel
free to give me a shout any time. Sound good?
HR Person: Yup!
BD Agent: Okay, great. Well, mate, I’ll get that email out to you straight away, and I look
forward to seeing you next week in person. Have a great day!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 4 --
